title: संकटनाशन गणेश स्तोत्र
devanagariText:
॥ श्री संकटनाशन गणेश स्तोत्रम् ॥

प्रणम्य शिरसा देवं गौरीपुत्रं विनायकम्।
भक्तावासं स्मरेन्नित्यमायुः कामार्थसिद्धये॥१॥

प्रथमं वक्रतुण्डं च एकदन्तं द्वितीयकम्।
तृतीयं कृष्णपिङ्गाक्षं गजवक्त्रं चतुर्थकम्॥२॥

लम्बोदरं पञ्चमं च षष्ठं विकटमेव च।
सप्तमं विघ्नराजेन्द्रं धूम्रवर्णं तथाष्टमम्॥३॥

नवमं भालचन्द्रं च दशमं तु विनायकम्।
एकादशं गणपतिं द्वादशं तु गजाननम्॥४॥

द्वादशैतानि नामानि त्रिसन्ध्यं यः पठेन्नरः।
न च विघ्नभयं तस्य सर्वसिद्धिकरं प्रभो॥५॥

विद्यार्थी लभते विद्यां धनार्थी लभते धनम्।
पुत्रार्थी लभते पुत्रान् मोक्षार्थी लभते गतिम्॥६॥

जपेद्गणपतिस्तोत्रं षड्भिर्मासैः फलं लभेत्।
संवत्सरेण सिद्धिं च लभते नात्र संशयः॥७॥

अष्टभ्यो ब्राह्मणेभ्यश्च लिखित्वा यः समर्पयेत्।
तस्य विद्या भवेत्सर्वा गणेशस्य प्रसादतः॥८॥

॥ इति श्री नारदपुराणे संकटनाशनं गणेशस्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Sankatanashana Ganesha Stotram ||

Pranamya Shirasa Devam Gauriputram Vinayakam.
Bhaktavasam Smarennityam Ayuh Kamarthasiddhaye.||1||

Prathamam Vakratundam Cha Ekadantam Dvitiyakam.
Tritiyam Krishnapingaksham Gajavaktram Chaturthakam.||2||

Lambodaram Panchamam Cha Shashtham Vikatameva Cha.
Saptamam Vighnarajendram Dhumravarnam Tathashtamam.||3||

Navamam Bhalachandram Cha Dashamam Tu Vinayakam.
Ekadasham Ganapatim Dvadasham Tu Gajananam.||4||

Dvadashaittani Namani Trisandhyam Yah Pathennnarah.
Na Cha Vighnabhayam Tasya Sarvasiddhikaram Prabho.||5||

Vidyarthi Labhate Vidyam Dhanarthi Labhate Dhanam.
Putrarthi Labhate Putran Moksharthi Labhate Gatim.||6||

Japedganapatistotram Shadbhirmasaih Phalam Labhet.
Samvatsarena Siddhim Cha Labhate Natra Sanshayah.||7||

Ashtabhyo Brahmanebhyashcha Likhitva Yah Samarpayet.
Tasya Vidya Bhavet Sarva Ganeshasya Prasadatah.||8||

|| Iti Shri Naradapurane Sankatanashanam Ganeshastotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
गौरीपुत्र विनायक देव को शीश झुकाकर प्रणाम करके, भक्तों के हृदय में बसने वाले उन गणेश जी का नित्य स्मरण करना चाहिए, जो आयु, इच्छा और अर्थ की सिद्धि देने वाले हैं।

उनके बारह नाम हैं - (1) वक्रतुण्ड, (2) एकदन्त, (3) कृष्णपिङ्गाक्ष, (4) गजवक्त्र, (5) लम्बोदर, (6) विकट, (7) विघ्नराजेन्द्र, (8) धूम्रवर्ण, (9) भालचन्द्र, (10) विनायक, (11) गणपति, (12) गजानन।

जो मनुष्य इन बारह नामों का तीनों सन्ध्याओं (प्रातः, मध्याह्न, सायं) में पाठ करता है, उसे विघ्नों का भय नहीं रहता और सभी कार्य सिद्ध होते हैं। विद्यार्थी को विद्या, धनार्थी को धन, पुत्रार्थी को पुत्र और मोक्षार्थी को सद्गति प्राप्त होती है। छह माह तक इस स्तोत्र का जाप करने से फल प्राप्त होता है और एक वर्ष में निश्चित रूप से सिद्धि मिलती है।

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीसंकटनाशनगणेशस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य नारद ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीगणपतिर्देवता। गं बीजम्। गौरीपुत्र शक्तिः। संकटनिवारणार्थे गणेशस्तोत्रजपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Traditionally recited for removal of all obstacles and calamities (sankat) from life
Invokes fulfilment of desires - knowledge, wealth, progeny, and moksha
Recitation at three sandhyas (dawn, noon, dusk) removes all fear of obstacles
Six months of regular recitation yields visible results
One year of continuous recitation bestows complete siddhi
One of the most potent short Ganesha prayers from Narada Purana
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Ganesha, Sankat Nashan Ganesh Stotra (संकटनाशन गणेश स्तोत्र) stands as a sacred prayer. Attributed to Narada Purana, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Devotees recite this prayer to destroy all obstacles and calamities (sankat) from life and recitation at three sandhyas (dawn, noon, dusk) removes all fear of obstacles. It is also said to grant fulfilment of desires - knowledge, wealth, progeny, and moksha. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Wednesday (Budhvar) and during Ganesh Chaturthi. Comprising 8 verses, it can be completed in approximately 5 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Sankat Nashan Ganesh Stotra in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Sankat Nashan Ganesh Stotra with Hindi meaning and transliteration. 12 names of Ganesha from Narada Purana for removal of all obstacles.

seoFaqs.question: Where in the Puranic corpus does Sankat Nashan Ganesh Stotra originate?
seoFaqs.answer: The hymn is preserved in the Narada Purana, narrated by the celestial sage Narada to a circle of disciples seeking a compact Ganesha prayer that could neutralise calamity. The opening line 'Pranamya shirasa devam Gauri-putram Vinayakam' frames the prayer as a salutation by one bowing the head before Ganesha, the son of Gauri. Its authorship within the Narada Purana places it among the earliest fixed Ganesha hymns and predates the better-known Ganapati Atharvashirsha. The text comprises eight verses, the central four enumerating the twelve sacred names of Ganesha, and is universally classified under Sankashti remedy literature. (Sources: Vaidika Vignanam; Stotranidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the twelve names of Ganesha enumerated in this stotra?
seoFaqs.answer: The canonical twelve names are: Sumukha (the lovely-faced), Ekadanta (single-tusked), Kapila (tawny), Gajakarnaka (elephant-eared), Lambodara (large-bellied), Vikata (formidable), Vighna-nashana (destroyer of obstacles), Vinayaka (foremost leader), Dhumraketu (smoke-bannered), Ganadhyaksha (lord of ganas), Bhalachandra (one bearing the moon on the forehead) and Gajanana (elephant-faced). The stotra promises that whoever chants these twelve names morning, noon and evening (trikalam) is freed from impediment of every kind, with sustained recitation conferring success in study, marriage, kingdom and detachment respectively. (Sources: Devshoppe; Vaidika Vignanam.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is the trikalam (three-time-daily) recitation specifically prescribed?
seoFaqs.answer: Each of the three sandhyas (junctures of dawn, midday and dusk) is considered a moment when the energetic threshold between human and divine realms is most permeable. By aligning recitation with these three sandhyas, the devotee inserts Ganesha's name into the day's natural rhythm, ensuring that no segment of activity (early morning study and travel, midday work and decisions, evening rest and family) proceeds without invocation of the obstacle-remover. The stotra itself promises specific, graded fruits at each level of regularity: removal of fear in seven days, fulfilment of desires in three weeks, freedom from bondage and ultimately moksha in six months of sustained trikalam recitation. (Sources: GuruKripa; Vaidika Vignanam.)

seoFaqs.question: Is Sankat Nashan Ganesh Stotra primarily a Sankashti Chaturthi recitation?
seoFaqs.answer: It is the most popular Sankashti Chaturthi recitation, but its scope is broader. Sankashti Chaturthi (the fourth lunar day of every Krishna paksha) is dedicated to Sankashtahara Ganapati, and the stotra's title Sankata-Nashana directly mirrors the Sankashtahara epithet, making it the natural fit. However the stotra is equally recited on Vinayaka Chaturthi (Shukla paksha), on Ganesha Jayanti, Ganesh Chaturthi, Angarki Chaturthi (when Sankashti falls on Tuesday), at the start of any new project, before examinations, and at the housewarming of a new home. (Sources: Stotranidhi; learncarnaticmusicblog.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of the closing phala-shruti verses?
seoFaqs.answer: The phala-shruti (statement of fruits) verses at the close declare that one who recites the stotra continuously for one day is freed from sorrow, for two months from disease, for six months attains desired siddhi, and for one year experiences moksha. The text further specifies categories of fruit by intent: students recite for vidya-prapti (gain of learning), the bound for vimukta (freedom), the ill for nirogata (health), the businessman for dhana (wealth) and the seeker for pravara-vakti (excellent speech). These graded promises are characteristic of Puranic stotra-phalashruti and signal the prayer's all-purpose remedial design. (Sources: Devshoppe; Pillai Center.)

seoFaqs.question: How should the stotra be combined with the broader Ganesha puja?
seoFaqs.answer: The standard sequence on Sankashti is: pre-dawn snan, sankalpa stating the date, place and intent, sthapana of a Ganesha murti or supari (areca nut) representing Ganesha, panchopachara (water, sandal paste, durva grass, modaka and lamp), recitation of Ganesha Atharvashirsha (if undertaken) followed by the Sankat Nashan Ganesh Stotra trikalam, and a moonrise-evening puja with offering of twenty-one durva blades and twenty-one modakas. The fast (vrata) is broken only after sighting the moon and offering arghya. The stotra anchors the verbal limb of this otherwise material puja and is regarded as essential for the vrata's completion. (Sources: GuruKripa; Stotranidhi.)
